input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's a complete `AppShell.xaml` setup:

**AppShell.xaml**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyApp.Views"
    Title="MyApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, top-level flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — single page, top-level flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
```csharp
namespace MyApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail page route so you can navigate with:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(ProductDetailsPage));
        // or with a parameter:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Views.ProductDetailsPage), typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Key points:**

- **`FlyoutItem`** = top-level menu entry. Wrapping `Tab` elements inside it gives you bottom tabs within that section.
- **`ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate`** = lazy loading — pages are created only when first visited.
- **`Routing.RegisterRoute`** is needed for pages *not* in the visual hierarchy (like detail/push pages). Navigate to it with `Shell.Current.GoToAsync`.
- To receive the `id` query parameter in `ProductDetailsPage`, decorate it with `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` or implement `IQueryAttributable`.
